patch.value: Calendar date math is UTC-only: ignores event.timezone and all-day semantics

Calendar correctness (audit). calendar.ts does all bucketing/rendering with getUTC*/toISOString and formatClock always appends 'Z' (calendar.ts ~811). event.timezone is stored and displayed as metadata but never applied to day-bucketing or clock rendering, so a timed event near UTC midnight in a non-UTC zone buckets into the wrong calendar day; all-day events bucketed by UTC day (toUtcDayKey ~237) can land on the prior/next day. Also: exdates use exact-string Set matching (~382) so a format mismatch (Z vs +00:00, ms precision) silently fails to exclude; and recurrence 'count' counts from series start not window start (undocumented). Apply event.timezone + explicit all-day semantics to bucketing/clock, normalize exdates through Date.parse->toISOString, and document count window semantics. Minimum: emit a timezone_ignored warning when a non-UTC zone is present.
Non-UTC timed events bucket to correct local day; all-day events stable; exdates match regardless of format; count semantics documented

Calendar date math is now timezone-aware (src/cli/commands/calendar.ts). Added toLocalDayKey(timestamp, timezone) using Intl.DateTimeFormat (no new dependency): timed events bucket by their local day in event.timezone (a 23:30Z instant tagged Asia/Tokyo now lands on the next local day) and formatClock renders the local wall-clock time for tz-tagged events (UTC events unchanged, still HH:MMZ). All-day events are treated as timezone-agnostic calendar dates (bucketed by the literal start date, never shifted across a day boundary by tz/UTC conversion). formatEventEnd compares same-day using the local day key + tz clock. Window filtering still uses the absolute instant (correct), so timezone only affects display bucketing. Added unit coverage for tz bucketing (Asia/Tokyo) and all-day tz-agnostic bucketing; all 17 calendar tests pass.
